meta.note: Single source of truth for the skill exam. Every must_include claim is sourced from a verified reference file (see `source`). Do NOT add a claim you cannot trace to a reference or installed typings — a wrong answer key silently inverts the eval. must_not entries are regexes that ONLY appear in wrong answers (safe for a fast pre-fail); semantic negatives are encoded as positive must_include claims instead.

axes.api: API understanding — signatures, what exists / what was removed
axes.react: Solid vs React — does the model drag React reflexes over
axes.v1: Solid 2.0 vs 1.x — does the model fall back to 1.x APIs

questions.title: createEffect signature
questions.prompt: Describe the signature of `createEffect`: how many callbacks it takes, what each one is for, and what happens if you pass a single callback. One short paragraph plus a 3-line example.
must_include: createEffect takes TWO functions: a compute and an apply (plus an optional options object)
compute runs tracked (records reactive deps) and returns a value; apply runs the side effect, is untracked, and may return a cleanup function
the single-callback form (one function only) throws / is invalid in 2.0

questions.title: reads lag writes
questions.prompt: Right after calling `setCount(1)`, what does `count()` return on the very next line, and why? How do you force the new value to be readable synchronously?
must_include: count() still returns the OLD value (0) on the next line — writes are batched on a microtask, reads return the last committed value
flush() drains the queue synchronously so the read sees the new value

questions.title: async data without createResource
questions.prompt: How do you load data asynchronously by id and render it while showing a spinner during loading? Name the primitive and the boundary.
must_include: async is just a computation: createMemo(() => fetchX(id())) returns a Promise, and consumers read the accessor normally (the 'no createResource' negative is enforced by the FORBIDDEN list, not this claim)
wrap consumers in a <Loading fallback={...}> boundary; loading state is structural, not a T|undefined hole

questions.title: isRefreshing removed in beta.15
questions.prompt: Can you import `isRefreshing` from `solid-js` in beta.15 to show a 'refreshing…' indicator? If not, what do you use instead?
must_include: isRefreshing is GONE from solid-js public exports as of beta.15 (it was public beta.0-beta.14, removed in beta.15) — do not import it
there is no drop-in public replacement; use isPending(() => x()) and/or <Loading> to observe readiness

questions.title: measure-then-position (createRenderEffect)
questions.prompt: You portal a tooltip panel into document.body and must measure it with getBoundingClientRect() then set its top/left. Which effect do you use, and where do you read the geometry — inside the `ref` callback or somewhere else? Why is the measurement correct?
must_include: use createRenderEffect (the render-lane / useLayoutEffect analog) for measure-then-write-layout

do NOT read getBoundingClientRect in the ref callback (the node may be pre-insert / pre-layout there); stash the node in a signal via ref={setNode} and read geometry inside the effect keyed on that signal
by the time the effect runs in the flush the node is already inserted into the DOM, so getBoundingClientRect reads true geometry — NOT zeros

questions.title: paint timing — no flash
questions.prompt: If you used createEffect instead of createRenderEffect to position that tooltip, would the user see a visible flash of the panel at the wrong spot before it jumps into place? Explain the paint timing precisely, and give the real reason to prefer the render lane.
must_include: NO visible flash — both the render lane and the user lane run in the SAME microtask flush, before the browser paints
createEffect is NOT post-paint like React's useEffect; Solid has no post-paint effect phase
the real reason to prefer createRenderEffect for layout is PHASE CORRECTNESS (lockstep with DOM, before user-lane consumers, not deferred by Suspense/hydration), not avoiding a flicker

questions.title: list rendering with For
questions.prompt: Render a list of todos keyed by identity. Then show how you would render it index-keyed (the old <Index> behavior). Note the callback shapes.
must_include: use <For each={...}> — default/keyed={true} keys by identity, callback is (rawItem, indexAccessor) so the index is called i()
index-keyed is <For keyed={false}> with callback (itemAccessor, plainIndex) — item is called item(), index is a plain number

questions.title: store nested update
questions.prompt: Update a nested store field `user.name` and push an item to `list` in one store. Show the import and the setter call.
must_include: store APIs (createStore, etc.) import from 'solid-js' — the solid-js/store subpath is gone
setter takes a draft you mutate in place: setStore(s => { s.user.name = 'B'; s.list.push(x); }) — produce is the default, no produce() import

questions.title: props are not destructured
questions.prompt: Write a component that receives a `title` prop and renders it in an <h1>. Call out any reactivity pitfall a React developer would hit.
must_include:
read props.title INSIDE the JSX — function Good(props) { return

{props.title}

}
do NOT destructure props in the parameter list and do NOT read props at the top level of the body — it captures once, goes stale, and warns STRICT_READ_UNTRACKED

questions.title: component runs once, no re-render
questions.prompt: In Solid 2.0, how many times does a component function execute, and when do effects re-run relative to 'renders'? Contrast explicitly with React's re-render-on-every-update model.
must_include: the component function runs exactly ONCE — there is no re-render and no virtual DOM
effects re-run only when their tracked dependencies change; the effect's compute phase IS the dependency declaration (no deps array)
questions.source: SKILL.md (deps-array model error); reviewer SKILL.md React-isms

questions.title: pass a signal to a child
questions.prompt: Given `const [count, setCount] = createSignal(0)`, pass count to a child <Counter> that displays it. Show both the call site and how the child reads it.
must_include: call the accessor at the JSX boundary: <Counter value={count()} /> — pass the VALUE, not the accessor function
the child reads props.value inside JSX (no destructuring)

questions.title: translate useState + useEffect
questions.prompt: Translate this React snippet to idiomatic Solid 2.0: `const [n, setN] = useState(0); useEffect(() => { document.title = String(n); }, [n]);`
must_include: useState(0) becomes createSignal(0)
useEffect becomes the SPLIT createEffect: createEffect(() => n(), v => { document.title = String(v); }) — compute reads n(), apply does the side effect
the dependency array [n] disappears — the compute phase tracks deps automatically

questions.title: composable naming — create* vs use*
questions.prompt: In Solid, what is the difference between naming a composable `createX` versus `useX` (for example `createPrefersDark` vs `usePrefersDark`)? When do you pick each?
must_include: createX creates a NEW reactive primitive owned by/scoped to the calling reactive scope — a fresh independent instance per call (the default)

useX accesses something already created and SHARED, not a fresh instance: either a context (useContext) or the singleton variant of a createX primitive (created once in a singleton/detached root and reused across callers)
questions.source: verified from @solid-primitives source: useWindowSize = createHydratableSingletonRoot(createWindowSize); usePrefersDark = createHydratableSingletonRoot(createPrefersDark); rationale solidjs/solid#317

questions.title: batch removed
questions.prompt: Batch these two writes together in Solid 2.0: `setA(1); setB(2);`. Show the idiomatic code.
must_include: batch() does NOT exist in 2.0 — batching is the default, so you just write setA(1); setB(2); with no wrapper
flush() is only needed if you must synchronously read your own writes right after

questions.title: onMount equivalent
questions.prompt: What is the Solid 2.0 equivalent of `onMount` for component setup that adds a window resize listener and removes it on cleanup? Show it.
must_include: use onSettled(() => { ...; return cleanup }) — it replaces onMount and supports a returned cleanup
onSettled is a leaf owner: you cannot create reactive primitives or call onCleanup inside it — return a cleanup function instead

questions.title: imports moved
questions.prompt: In Solid 2.0, where do `render`, `Portal`, and `createStore` come from? And what replaced `classList`?
must_include: render and Portal import from '@solidjs/web' (NOT solid-js/web)
createStore (and other store APIs) import from 'solid-js' (NOT solid-js/store)
classList is gone — use the object/array form of the class prop: class={{ active: isActive() }}

questions.title: createSelector replacement
questions.prompt: You need a derived store that marks the selected row id without notifying every row in the list. Which 1.x API did this, and what replaces it in 2.0?
must_include: the 1.x API was createSelector — it is gone in 2.0
the replacement is createProjection (a readonly derived store whose derive fn mutates a draft), which updates fine-grained without notifying the whole collection
